good: Calorie ring card is well-proportioned and readable at 375px
issues: Action bar (Log Food + icons + weight input) overflows horizontally — weight input and additional buttons are clipped off-screen
Right-side streak/BMI card is partially cut off, content cramped in narrow column
Two-column card layout shrinks both cards too narrow for comfortable reading on SE
fixes: Wrap action bar with flex-wrap: wrap so weight input drops to next line on 375px
Stack the two dashboard cards to single column (flex-direction: column) below 400px breakpoint
Add overflow-x: hidden to page container to prevent horizontal scroll

good: Today's Macros section is clean and fully visible below the fold — good progressive disclosure
issues: Action bar still clips the last button/icon at 390px — rightmost element partially off-screen
Right BMI card top section has excessive empty space before the flame icon appears, making the card feel unbalanced
Weight input field text 'Weight (kg)' placeholder may render below 14px on this viewport
fixes: Set action bar max-width: 100% with overflow-x: hidden and flex-wrap: wrap
Add padding-top to BMI card content or vertically center its inner content with justify-content: center
Ensure input placeholder font-size is explicitly set to min 14px

good: Next milestone banner with green styling looks polished and contextually useful
issues: Action bar overflows — 'Log' button and avatar 'D' are clipped at right edge on 412px
Two-column card layout leaves the right streak/BMI card with too little horizontal space, content feels squeezed
Water Intake section partially cut off at bottom — suggests no graceful scroll affordance
fixes: Constrain action bar to viewport width using width: 100%; overflow: hidden or move Log button + avatar to a second row
Increase right card min-width or switch to single column at ≤430px
Add padding-bottom: 80px to page container to ensure last card is fully scrollable into view

good: Full action bar renders completely with all elements visible — Log Food, icons, weight input, Log button, avatar all fit cleanly
issues: Content is left-aligned in a narrow centered column leaving large black empty margins on both sides — wasted screen real estate on 768px tablet
Two dashboard cards don't expand to use available width — right BMI card looks undersized relative to viewport
Typography hierarchy between card headers and body text could be more differentiated at tablet size
fixes: Set dashboard container max-width to 90% or 700px on tablet and center it, expanding card grid to fill
Use CSS grid with grid-template-columns: 2fr 1fr on ≥768px to better proportion the two cards
Increase card header font-size by 2–4px on tablet breakpoint for better visual hierarchy

good: Desktop navigation bar is clean with full Log Food button, icons, weight input and user avatar all properly spaced
issues: Dashboard content is rendered in a very narrow centered column — roughly 700px wide on a 1280px screen, leaving ~280px of black void on each side
The two-column card layout (calorie + streak) is functional but undersized — cards could expand significantly on desktop
No desktop-optimised layout — it appears to be a mobile layout simply centred, not a true responsive desktop design
fixes: Increase container max-width to 900–1000px on ≥1024px breakpoint
Consider a 3-column grid on desktop: calorie card | macros | streak/BMI side panel
Add a sidebar or secondary content panel on desktop to better utilise horizontal space and elevate the app to a premium desktop experience
